seoTitle: Pampas vs Paradise Point — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Pampas and Paradise Point. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Pampas vs Paradise Point: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Pampas and Paradise Point.
heading: Pampas vs Paradise Point — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Pampas, Queensland and Paradise Point, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Paradise Point has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Pampas (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Pampas or Paradise Point safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Pampas has a flood risk score of 36/100, while Paradise Point scores 30/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Pampas scores 56/100 for bushfire risk. Paradise Point scores 47/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Pampas or Paradise Point?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Paradise Point has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.15% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
